desc: Tallinn blends a storybook medieval core with modern Nordic-Baltic creative districts and a compact seafront layout that is easy to explore without long transfers. The city is most comfortable for long outdoor days from late May to early September, while December adds festive atmosphere and stronger accommodation pressure.
hls: Compact city ideal for 2–4 day trips
Strong digital infrastructure and remote-work ease
Big seasonal daylight contrast on the Gulf of Finland

todo.name: Old Town upper/lower loop
todo.desc: Walk Toompea viewpoints, city walls, and lower-town squares for Tallinn's signature medieval skyline.

todo.name: Telliskivi creative quarter
todo.desc: Browse design shops, food halls, and street art in repurposed industrial spaces.

todo.name: Seaplane Harbour museum
todo.desc: Use this interactive museum to connect Tallinn's naval and Baltic-sea story.

todo.name: Kalamaja + Noblessner walk
todo.desc: Combine wooden-house streets with modern waterfront redevelopment and cafés.

todo.name: Kadriorg & KUMU
todo.desc: Pair imperial-era park grounds with Estonia's leading art museum.

todo.name: Seasonal planning block
todo.desc: In Nov–Jan, front-load outdoor sights in midday light and reserve evenings for saunas and dining.

wifi.note: Excellent citywide connectivity with broad laptop-friendly café coverage; Estonia remains one of Europe's easiest digital-work bases.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick-Tallinn is often available nonstop with seasonal frequency shifts. Return fares are often ~£40-£110 off-peak, ~£110-£190 in shoulder demand, and ~£190+ in midsummer or December festive peaks.
prac.visa: Estonia is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: November-January daylight is very limited; front-load outdoor sights near midday.
Winter pavements can be icy in old-town lanes — use footwear with good grip.
Cruise-ship days can create short-lived midday crowd spikes in the medieval core.

sweet: June-August is usually best for long daylight and outdoor city walks, while September can offer better value with still-pleasant conditions.
